currentFocus: Crosswalk one authentic subsystem across the Steam binary, pinned Stuart source, pinned AYA exporter, and rebuild, then implement only the accepted behavior contract.

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

retainedStaticAccounting.status: Retained as prior static accounting pending a deep quality audit; these counters are not runtime, signature, semantic, gameplay, or rebuild proof.

auditVerdict.maturity: Approximately 2.5/5: mature static cataloguing and selectively strong subsystem analysis, but not binary-wide semantic completion.
auditVerdict.claimBoundary: The claim that every function is fully reverse engineered is not supported. The defensible claim is that a historical local snapshot reportedly satisfied a narrow name/comment/signature hygiene contract.
auditVerdict.reproducibility: The tracked checkout does not contain the current functions_quality.tsv needed to re-prove the historical 6411-function snapshot.
auditVerdict.principalRisk: Automation optimized placeholder-name and comment metrics faster than owner, ABI, type, semantics, provenance, and runtime confidence were established.

currentTruth: The repository contains extensive source-derived and Ghidra-derived function documentation, ledgers, checks, and wave artifacts.
A complete clean-room Battle Engine Aquila runtime, renderer, gameplay loop, or parity demo does not yet exist.
Stuart's Onslaught source is architecture and naming evidence; Steam retail binary, real files, and runtime read-back remain higher authority.

references/Onslaught is pinned at 792545b996365f383781c666d145ea6cbda83f3a and references/AYAResourceExtractor at 6f3df296201ecc62bc09c39f7a93d8a4fb2f1638; provenance, licenses, build posture, and capability gaps require a fresh inventory.
Binary review, source crosswalk, and exporter coverage may proceed in parallel; rebuild implementation consumes only a separately accepted evidence contract and feeds ambiguities back to RE.
No live Ghidra mutation or copied-runtime proof is authorized by the current baseline slice.
boundaries: Keep the installed game and original BEA.exe read-only.
Do not commit copied executable bytes, extracted assets, full Ghidra databases, raw debugger logs, screenshots/frame dumps, or private proof roots.
Ghidra mutation requires a separate structured baton, exclusive lease, backup, dry run, serialized write/read-back, and ledger update.
Any future strict clean-room lane must not expose implementation agents to source or decompilation material; all rebuild code must avoid proprietary code and payloads and carry explicit provenance.
activeBlockers: A fresh whole-binary denominator and current complete export are required before another closure claim.
Prior 100 percent counters measure review and hygiene accounting, not binary-wide semantic completion.
A defensible clean-room label requires implementation-room separation and license review; near-term code must use an RE-informed provenance label.
nextSteps: Inventory the two pinned reference submodules: fork/upstream provenance, license, build/test posture, source ownership, exporter format coverage, and known gaps.
Select one recognizable subsystem and review its 5-20-function Steam call-chain cluster under the measured quality rubric.
Crosswalk source owners and exporter/asset dependencies against Steam static/runtime evidence, recording every disagreement instead of choosing by intuition.
Freeze an accepted public-safe behavior contract and implement that authentic slice in deterministic Core plus the Godot adapter with headless and native evidence.
